fields.description:
## Summary
Build the FOUNDATION of epic 3006-e198: the pure classifier, the per-binding baseline, the decision-census telemetry, the circuit breaker, and the test harness. This ticket wires NOTHING into the live pass (the four drift-class bugs and the rollout task consume it). All invariants are specified by ADRs 0026-0029 (docs/adr/) — read them FIRST.

## New module: src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/classify.py (pure leaf; no I/O, no imports from differ/outbound_differ/inbound_differ)
- `class ObservedJira(Enum): PRESENT | CONFIRMED_404 | ABSENT_IN_WINDOW | TRANSPORT_ERROR` — the four-way observation state of ADR 0028 (NEVER a boolean; ABSENT_IN_WINDOW must never be treated as deleted).
- `class LocalState(Enum): ACTIVE | TERMINAL | ABSENT` — TERMINAL = local status in {archived, deleted} or archived flag.
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class Decision: kind: DecisionKind; reason: str; payload: dict` — kind is a CLOSED enum: `SYNC_FIELDS | ADOPT | TERMINAL_TRANSITION | PROBE_GET | RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE | SKIP_RETIRED | ALERT | NOOP`.
- `def classify(local: dict|None, jira: ObservedJira-tagged snapshot value, binding: dict|None, baseline: dict|None) -> Decision` — the FULL (LocalState x ObservedJira x binding-present) matrix, every cell explicitly enumerated (no default-else fallthrough: unmatched cell raises).
- Matrix anchor cells (must match the 2026-07-03 spike + the child bugs): bound+ACTIVE+PRESENT -> SYNC_FIELDS; bound+TERMINAL+PRESENT(not Done) -> TERMINAL_TRANSITION; bound+*+ABSENT_IN_WINDOW -> PROBE_GET; bound+*+CONFIRMED_404 (grace met) -> RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE; bound+*+TRANSPORT_ERROR -> NOOP(defer); unbound+PRESENT+retired -> SKIP_RETIRED; unbound+PRESENT -> ADOPT; bound+ABSENT-local -> ALERT.

## Per-binding baseline (ADR 0026 — read its 4 decision points verbatim)
- Extend the bindings.json entry (binding_store.py, `BindingStore`): add `baseline: {summary, description, priority, status, assignee}` + `baseline_advanced_at`; bump the file `version` field; older entries without baseline are VALID (absent baseline degrades to local-wins per ADR 0026 #2).
- New methods `get_baseline(local_id) -> dict|None`, `set_baseline(local_id, fields: dict)`; persisted by the existing `save()` + committed by the existing `_commit_binding_store_snapshot` (reconcile.py) — no new commit path.
- One-shot seed helper `seed_baselines_from_snapshot(prev_snapshot: dict)` — sets a baseline for every bound key present in the snapshot (derisk X4 proved all 613 bound+present keys carry all 5 fields). Do NOT delete prev_snapshot or change its consumers in this ticket.
- Corrupt bindings.json stays FAIL-CLOSED (existing behavior); do not weaken it.

## Circuit breaker (pure function here; live wiring is the rollout task)
- `def check_blast_radius(decisions: Iterable[Decision], total_bindings: int, max_acting_fraction: float) -> BreakerVerdict` in classify.py or a sibling `breaker.py`; acting kinds = {TERMINAL_TRANSITION, RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE, ADOPT}; config key `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` default 0.10 (2026-07-03 census measured 1.14% acting — 8.8x headroom).

## Decision-census telemetry (approved remediation 1)
- `def census(decisions) -> dict` returning {counts-by-kind, acting_count, acting_pct, breaker_verdict}; emitted via the existing `SyncLogger.log("decision_census", **census)` (sync_logger.py) — the emit call itself lands with the first consumer, but the record shape + unit test land HERE.

## Tests (approved remediation 3 included)
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/classify/test_matrix.py — table test: EVERY matrix cell, incl. the ADR 0028 regression cells (Done-beyond-cap -> PROBE_GET not RETIRE; transport-error -> NOOP).
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/classify/test_properties.py — hypothesis: (a) convergence: apply decisions to a model world, re-classify -> zero acting; (b) no-orphan; (c) conservation (no double-bind); (d) direction-preservation: a Jira-side edit with local==baseline is never pushed outbound (ADR 0026 consequence).
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/classify/test_frozen_census.py — replay sanitized frozen fixtures (tests/fixtures/bridge_convergence/frozen-2026-07-03/: bindings.json + snapshot skeleton, PII/body-stripped) asserting EXACTLY 7 acting decisions (5x TERMINAL_TRANSITION REB-464/456/465/466/457, 1x PROBE_GET REB-530, 1x ADOPT REB-532) then fixed-point after simulated heal.
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_binding_baseline.py — get/set/seed/version-compat/corrupt-fail-closed.
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/classify/test_multipass_stateful.py — the epic's multi-pass stateful + fault-injection cell: a model world driven through N passes where the baseline is PERSISTED to a real temp bindings.json between passes (the faithful-persistence gap that hid drift class B); inject partial-apply failure (a Decision's apply raises mid-pass) and assert the NEXT pass re-derives the same Decision and self-heals to the fixed point.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] classify.py exists as a pure leaf module (no reconciler imports) with the closed Decision enum and the fully-enumerated matrix; unmatched cells raise.
- [ ] Baseline field + get/set/seed on BindingStore; absent baseline degrades local-wins; corrupt store still fails closed; version bumped with back-compat read.
- [ ] check_blast_radius + census implemented with unit tests; config key `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` (default 0.10) registered in the typed config schema (_config_schema.py).
- [ ] All five test files above pass; frozen-census fixture checked in (sanitized); hypothesis suite includes the four named properties; the multi-pass test persists the baseline through a real file between passes and asserts self-heal after injected partial failure.
- [ ] `make lint` + `make test` green; no file exceeds the 800 LOC soft cap.

## Implementation contract — resolved specifics (remediates plan-review 2026-07-03)

These pin the concrete decisions an executor needs (each was flagged as ambiguous
in the ticket text; all are now decided):

- **`jira` parameter type** — a frozen dataclass, NOT a loose "tagged value":
  `@dataclass(frozen=True) class JiraObservation: state: ObservedJira; key: str = "";
  fields: Mapping|None = None; retired: bool = False`. Matrix cells read
  `jira.state is ObservedJira.PRESENT`; `fields` is the snapshot value (present only
  when PRESENT); `retired` gates ADOPT vs SKIP_RETIRED for an unbound key.
- **Grace-met for `RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE`** — `classify(local, jira, binding, baseline,
  *, grace: int = 3)`: the grace threshold is a keyword-only arg (keeps the documented
  4-positional signature) so the function stays pure. On `CONFIRMED_404`, classify
  PREDICTS whether this miss crosses grace using `binding["absent_404_count"] + 1 >=
  grace`; below grace it returns `PROBE_GET` (keep counting), at/over grace
  `RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE`. `binding_store.note_absent` remains the sole ENACTOR of the
  increment+retire (classify predicts, note_absent enacts — no double count). Raw
  GET outcomes are mapped to `ObservedJira` by the caller (the live probe path;
  `inbound_probe`/`_safe_get_issue` sentinels → `CONFIRMED_404`/`TRANSPORT_ERROR`),
  never inside the pure classifier.
- **Acting kinds vs the "7 drift" frozen census** — the circuit breaker's ACTING set
  is `{TERMINAL_TRANSITION, RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE, ADOPT}` (PROBE_GET is a bounded READ,
  not acting). The frozen-census AC's "7" is the DRIFT/actionable set (5 TERMINAL +
  1 PROBE_GET REB-530 + 1 ADOPT); the test asserts those 7 decisions by key AND
  asserts `census(...).acting_count == 6` (breaker semantics). Both are consistent.
- **`BreakerVerdict`** — a frozen dataclass:
  `BreakerVerdict(allowed: bool, acting_count: int, total_bindings: int,
  acting_fraction: float, max_acting_fraction: float, reason: str)`.
  `total_bindings == 0` ⇒ allowed (nothing to gate; no divide-by-zero).
- **`reconciler.max_acting_fraction`** — registered in `_config_schema.py`
  `ReconcilerConfig` as `float = 0.10`, coercer `_as_float(v, k, minimum=0.0,
  maximum=1.0)`. A `reconciler.baseline_dual_write: bool = False` key is added
  alongside for the rollout's shadow phase.
- **Property tests use stdlib `random` (seeded), NOT `hypothesis`** — the repo
  declares no `hypothesis` dep and no test uses it; adding one would drift CI. The
  four named properties (convergence, no-orphan, conservation, direction-preservation)
  run as deterministic seeded-random model worlds.
- **Frozen fixture provenance** — `tests/fixtures/bridge_convergence/frozen-2026-07-03/`
  holds `bindings.json` + `snapshot.json` + `locals.json`, generated to reproduce the
  2026-07-03 census SHAPE (608 active + 5 archived-live + 1 dangling + 1 unbound =
  614 bindings / 614 issues / 615 cells); summaries/bodies are `[sanitized]` placeholders,
  assignees emptied — no PII.
- **`baseline` lifecycle** — the baseline lives ON the binding entry, so retirement
  (which removes the whole entry) removes it with the binding; no separate cleanup.
- **`seed_baselines_from_snapshot` re-run policy** — idempotent by construction: it
  re-derives each bound key's baseline from the passed snapshot (overwrite-with-same is
  a no-op for a stable snapshot); it never deletes.
- **decision_census record shape** — `{counts: {kind->int}, acting_count: int,
  acting_pct: float, total: int, breaker: {allowed, acting_fraction, max_acting_fraction,
  reason}}`; documented as a contract in the `census()` docstring.
- **800 LOC cap** — classify.py is ~380 LOC; the CI module-size gate
  (`.github/workflows/test.yml`) is the proving check.
- **ADRs 0026–0029** are committed under `docs/adr/` on this branch (the governing spec).
